main.name: Arthur De Los Santos
main.shortDesc: Candidate for S.B. and admitted to M.Eng. in Computer Science and Engineering at MIT

about.title: Computer Science & Engineering Student
about.about: Candidate for S.B. in Computer Science and Engineering (GPA: 4.9/5.0) and accepted to M.Eng. at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. Experienced in Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, C++, and ROS2.

educations.institute: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), Cambridge, MA
educations.degree: Candidate for S.B. in Computer Science and Engineering

educations.institute: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), Cambridge, MA
educations.degree: Accepted to M.Eng. in Computer Science and Engineering
desc: Relevant Coursework: Algorithms I and II, Software Construction, Machine Learning, Multivariable Calculus, Linear Algebra, Probability, Fundamentals of Statistics
Low-level Programming, Computation Structures, Computer Systems Engineering, Natural Language Processing, Advances in Computer Vision, Robotic Manipulation
Statistical Learning Theory, Numerical Algorithms for ML, Distributed Computer Systems Engineering, Computer Systems Security, Multimodal AI
Course Grader for Introduction to Statistical Data Analysis
Course Lab Assistant for Introduction to Machine Learning
Researched modern biosignal conversion methods, downselecting to a model for our specific health-related applications

Re-implemented transformer model from paper in TensorFlow; trained on standard dataset using MIT Lincoln Lab's GPU cluster, achieving 96%+ classification accuracy with 5-fold cross-validation for biosignal-based health diagnostics
Visualized model results to support publication and facilitate interpretation of biosignal classification performance

Conducted research in the application of deep learning for retina disease screening, optimizing and extending current machine learning algorithms using open-source Python libraries such as Keras and TensorFlow
Developed a ROS2/C++ tool that simulates noisy depth maps, enabling accurate and repeatable testing of object height estimation algorithms without physical hardware
Enhanced a ROS2-based object height detection algorithm to improve measurement accuracy and noise robustness

Trained and optimized object detection/segmentation models (e.g., SAM2) that improved segmentation recall by >10% at IoU>=0.90 and 0.95 thresholds, while satisfying inference speed and memory constraints for real-time deployment in autonomous warehouse robots

experiences.company: Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (MIT CSAIL)
desc: Conduct research on adaptive vision-language navigation systems that combine perception, reasoning, and human feedback efficiently to improve robustness and trust in real-world human-robot interaction
